'''Peter Lupton''' (born 7 March 1982) is a professional [[rugby football]]er who plays and coaches [[rugby league]] for the [[New York Knights (rugby league)|New York Knights]] in the [[North American Rugby League]] competition.

Lupton began his career playing rugby league as a {{rlp|lf|sr|so}}, or {{rlp|sh}} in [[England]] and [[Wales]]. He earned selection in the [[Wales national rugby league team|Welsh national team]] after qualifying through 3-years residency, and later captained the team at the [[2014 European Cup]]. Lupton moved to [[Boston]], [[United States]],{{year needed|date=May 2021}}<ref>{{cite web|last=Newsum|first=Matt|date=15 June 2016|title=Peter Lupton: The rugby league 'pioneer' living the American dream|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/36538569|work=BBC|access-date=23 May 2021}}</ref> and played [[rugby union]] professionally and rugby league as an amateur. He was named [[player-coach]] of the Boston Thirteens for their first season as a semi-professional club in 2021.

==Background==
Lupton was born in [[Whitehaven]], [[Cumbria]], England.

==Career==
===Rugby league===
He has previously played for [[Workington Town]] in [[RFL League 1|Championship 1]] and [[Crusaders Rugby League|Crusaders]] in the [[Super League]].

Whilst playing for the [[Castleford Tigers]] and the Crusaders, he played {{rlp|ce|so|sh}} or {{rlp|lf}}.

Lupton has previously played for the [[London Broncos]] and [[Hull FC]].

In March 2015, Lupton signed with the [[Barrow Raiders]]

In 2016, Lupton joined the [[Boston 13s]] in the [[USA Rugby League]]. He was named as coach in 2017,<ref>{{cite web|date=19 May 2017|title=Peter Lupton to coach Boston 13s in 2017|url=https://usarl.org/news/2017/peter-lupton-to-coach-boston-13s/|website=USARL.org|access-date=23 May 2021}}</ref> continuing the role in 2021 as the club joined the inaugural [[North American Rugby League]] season and turned semi-professional.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=29 April 2021|title=Boston Thirteens appoint former Wales captain Peter Lupton as player-coach|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/boston-thirteens-appoint-former-wales-captain-peter-lupton-as-player-coach/|access-date=29 April 2021}}</ref>

On 8 May 2026 it was reported that he had joined the reformed [[New York Knights (rugby league)|New York Knights]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|author=Aaron Bower|date=8 May 2026|title=Former Hull and Castleford star Peter Lupton lands new club – aged 44|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/former-hull-and-castleford-star-peter-lupton-lands-new-club-aged-44}}</ref>

===Rugby union===
In the fall of 2018, Lupton signed with the [[New England Free Jacks]] in the [[Major League Rugby|MLR]].

==International==
Lupton was named in the [[Wales national rugby league team|Welsh]] squad for the [[2011 Rugby League Four Nations|2011 Four Nations]], qualifying on 3-years residency.<ref>[http://www.rleague.com/content/article.php?id=41183 Wales release 35-man train-on squad] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111115170533/http://rleague.com/content/article.php?id=41183 |date=2011-11-15 }} ''rleague.com'', 14 September 2011</ref><ref>{{cite web|last=Wilson|first=Andy|date=18 October 2011|title=Matt Seamark, Tyson Frizell and Peter Lupton receive Wales calls|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2011/oct/17/seamark-frizell-lupton-wales|work=The Guardian|access-date=23 May 2021}}</ref> He made his début in a warm-up match against [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]], but did not feature in the tournament. 

Lupton played for {{rlnt|Wales}} at the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup|2013 World Cup]], and captained the side at the [[2014 European Cup]] in the absence of [[Craig Kopczak]].<ref>{{cite web|date=13 October 2014|title=2014 European Championships: Peter Lupton to lead Wales out against Scotland|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12040/9515237/2014-european-championships-peter-lupton-to-lead-wales-out-against-scotland|work=Sky Sports|access-date=23 May 2021}}</ref> In that tournament, Lupton captained the Welsh side in losses against {{rlnt|Scotland}}, {{rlnt|France}}, and {{rlnt|Ireland}}. 

On 1 Mar 2024 he was named in the {{rlnt|United States}} team for the test against {{rlnt|Canada}} staged in Las Vegas on 3 Mar 2024<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=1 Mar 2024|title=Ex-Super League stalwart in USA squad for Canada test ahead of NRL Las Vegas opener|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ex-super-league-stalwart-peter-lupton-in-usa-squad-for-canada-test-match-ahead-of-nrl-las-vegas-opener}}</ref> In November of 2024, Lupton was named in the {{rlnt|United States}} team for a two-test tour of {{rlnt|South Africa}}. <ref>{{Cite web |last=Bower |first=Aaron |date=2024-11-05 |title=Former NRL, Super League and WWE star earns USA rugby league call |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/former-salford-red-devils-winger-earns-shock-usa-call-up-in-rugby-league-return |access-date=2025-09-15 |website=www.loverugbyleague.com |language=en}}</ref>
